I mean
They use helot or para comp
They aim to win easily while disabling people cards
No fun in playing against a team where you can't play any cards at all
And they spam it to win
They go pretty competitively i would say
If all your cards are strikes you might have a problem right there
Not forcibly
You can have a shit draw early on
And all you draw is strike
Then they para you AoE
Your hand are 6/10 clogged
So you need to end turn for next turn to only draw 4 cards
Then they para again
Now 6 cards in your hands are illusion
4 playable but it might also be useless cards
You wasted 3 turns to finally draw something decent
And by that time they already made all of your team mid life
That's how para team works
That's just low rolling
It happen more often than you think
if you low roll that often you might wanna just not play games with any RNG in them

I do have skill issue imma lose my mind <:dead:1356082627509092372>

So as it stands, I have played fighting games starting 8 years ago. Never a hardcore player but also not so casual that I do not go beyond pressing buttons. It's quite unfortunate that your experience gave you the conclusion that newer fighting games end up as "rng" based, but if you had trouble explaining your concept to other players it would seem... that it's just you, no?
No, it's a difference in the landscape of the community
But as for traphase, yeah, definitely rng heavy no cap
If you lived in a forest all your life and are transplanted to a desert where people have only ever known desert, it's awfully hard to explain what a forest is
Uh... huh. So it would seem that while everyone is in the desert, you somehow ended up in a forest xD
Everyone was in the forest 20 years ago
that's the point
I guess that's the wonder of games, different people get different experiences~
Right, 20 years ago when games could actually bug out without fixes is somehow a less rng based game compared to modern ones
